posts.html:
We are living in a time of deep complexity. Climate crises, social inequalities, and growing disconnection shape the world that children and young adults are inheriting. These are not problems that can be solved with information alone. They ask for something deeper — the capacity to wonder, to imagine, to feel, to see connections, and to act with care.

Yet, much of education today moves in the opposite direction. It fragments knowledge, rushes time, and often overlooks the inner worlds of children — their curiosity, emotions, imagination, and sense of beauty.

We exist to nurture what is often left out.

For young children, this means creating spaces where they can touch, feel, move, and experience the world through their senses — where language grows from lived experience, where imagination is alive, and where beauty and creativity are not extras but essentials.

For young adults, it means engaging deeply with the world — understanding interconnectedness, holding multiple perspectives, asking critical questions, and taking thoughtful action. It means cultivating ecological sensitivity, emotional depth, and the ability to learn beyond classrooms, across life.

We believe that meaningful encounters with good children’s literature, nature, and interdisciplinary experiences can open these pathways. They help young people not just understand the world, but relate to it — with curiosity, care, and responsibility.

Khojbeen Mandali exists to create and hold such encounters.

What values and skills do we want to focus on ?

  • For young children : Sensorial engagement, Development of language, affective domain, esthetic sense and creativity
  • For young adults : Interconnectedness, System thinking, Multi-perspective, Critical inquiry and action, development of creativity, affective domain, esthetic sense and environmental consciousness, life-wide, informal, self-directed learning
posts.plaintext:
We are living in a time of deep complexity. Climate crises, social inequalities, and growing disconnection shape the world that children and young adults are inheriting. These are not problems that can be solved with information alone. They ask for something deeper — the capacity to wonder, to imagine, to feel, to see connections, and to act with care.

Yet, much of education today moves in the opposite direction. It fragments knowledge, rushes time, and often overlooks the inner worlds of children — their curiosity, emotions, imagination, and sense of beauty.

We exist to nurture what is often left out.

For young children, this means creating spaces where they can touch, feel, move, and experience the world through their senses — where language grows from lived experience, where imagination is alive, and where beauty and creativity are not extras but essentials.

For young adults, it means engaging deeply with the world — understanding interconnectedness, holding multiple perspectives, asking critical questions, and taking thoughtful action. It means cultivating ecological sensitivity, emotional depth, and the ability to learn beyond classrooms, across life.

We believe that meaningful encounters with good children’s literature, nature, and interdisciplinary experiences can open these pathways. They help young people not just understand the world, but relate to it — with curiosity, care, and responsibility.

Khojbeen Mandali exists to create and hold such encounters.


What values and skills do we want to focus on ?

 * For young children : Sensorial engagement, Development of language, affective domain, esthetic sense and creativity
 * For young adults : Interconnectedness, System thinking, Multi-perspective, Critical inquiry and action, development of creativity, affective domain, esthetic sense and environmental consciousness, life-wide, informal, self-directed learning

posts.title: Nidhi Pal
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Nidhi Pal is a science educator and nature enthusiast who enjoys facilitating unconventional learning experiences with children through inquiry-based experiments, projects, and conversations. Over the past five years, she has worked closely with children and teachers, creating spaces where curiosity and exploration can flourish. She is particularly interested in place-based education, climate literacy, and socio-emotional learning, and continues to explore ways of integrating these into meaningful learning experiences.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html: Nidhi Pal is a science educator and nature enthusiast who enjoys facilitating unconventional learning experiences with children through inquiry-based experiments, projects, and conversations. Over the past five years, she has worked closely with children and teachers, creating spaces where curiosity and exploration can flourish. She is particularly interested in place-based education, climate literacy, and socio-emotional learning, and continues to explore ways of integrating these into meaningful learning experiences.
posts.plaintext: Nidhi Pal is a science educator and nature enthusiast who enjoys facilitating unconventional learning experiences with children through inquiry-based experiments, projects, and conversations. Over the past five years, she has worked closely with children and teachers, creating spaces where curiosity and exploration can flourish. She is particularly interested in place-based education, climate literacy, and socio-emotional learning, and continues to explore ways of integrating these into meaningful learning experiences.
posts.custom_excerpt: Capacity building, Resource development, Learning design

posts.title: Bansi
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Bansi is an Educator, who besides painting at whim and long naps on the weekends, is passionate towards literature in languages and children’s right to access the written world in their own language. Her work in Education focuses on creating spaces for affective engagement and affective development of children through language and art.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html: Bansi is an Educator, who besides painting at whim and long naps on the weekends, is passionate towards literature in languages and children’s right to access the written world in their own language. Her work in Education focuses on creating spaces for affective engagement and affective development of children through language and art.
posts.plaintext: Bansi is an Educator, who besides painting at whim and long naps on the weekends, is passionate towards literature in languages and children’s right to access the written world in their own language. Her work in Education focuses on creating spaces for affective engagement and affective development of children through language and art.

posts.title: Mihir Pathak
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Mihir Pathak is an educator who designs and facilitates experiential learning journeys for children, young adults, and educators. His work draws on experiential methodologies such as theatre, nature-based explorations, and interdisciplinary projects to nurture affective development & ecological consciousnesses. Over the years, he has worked across diverse learning environments, collaborating with educators, artists, and researchers to explore alternative approaches to learning and education.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html: Mihir Pathak is an educator who designs and facilitates experiential learning journeys for children, young adults, and educators. His work draws on experiential methodologies such as theatre, nature-based explorations, and interdisciplinary projects to nurture affective development & ecological consciousnesses. Over the years, he has worked across diverse learning environments, collaborating with educators, artists, and researchers to explore alternative approaches to learning and education.
posts.plaintext: Mihir Pathak is an educator who designs and facilitates experiential learning journeys for children, young adults, and educators. His work draws on experiential methodologies such as theatre, nature-based explorations, and interdisciplinary projects to nurture affective development & ecological consciousnesses. Over the years, he has worked across diverse learning environments, collaborating with educators, artists, and researchers to explore alternative approaches to learning and education.

posts.title: Patarango - Magazine
posts.lexical: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"(For young readers up to 9yr old)","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"children":[],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Patarango","type":"extended-text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":" is a Gujarati magazine for young readers (5 to 10yrs) filled with joy & sparks:  poems, stories, diary, letters, travelogues and features on science, nature, art…material for every aspect of life.","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Every issue gathers small moments of curiosity, discovery, and wonder","type":"extended-text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1},{"type":"button","version":1,"buttonText":"Read more - Concept note of magazine","alignment":"left","buttonUrl":"https://docs.google.com/document/d/1MzVtNukyQbMG_6PnDxFYm1WF6WOzAroi28DDNRK41j4/edit?usp=drive_link"},{"children":[],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
posts.html:
(For young readers up to 9yr old)

Patarango is a Gujarati magazine for young readers (5 to 10yrs) filled with joy & sparks:  poems, stories, diary, letters, travelogues and features on science, nature, art…material for every aspect of life.

Every issue gathers small moments of curiosity, discovery, and wonder

Read more - Concept note of magazine
posts.plaintext:
(For young readers up to 9yr old)



Patarango is a Gujarati magazine for young readers (5 to 10yrs) filled with joy & sparks:  poems, stories, diary, letters, travelogues and features on science, nature, art…material for every aspect of life.

Every issue gathers small moments of curiosity, discovery, and wonder

Read more - Concept note of magazine

posts.html:
• Food Camp : 3 day residential camp with children to explore science, sociology, art - culture of central theme Food. Transdisciplinary Approach
  • Voice of Vishwamitri : Group of teen age children studied Vishwamitri river in Vadodara, Gujarat and presented their work in Climate Festival, BLR as Geo inquiry Project.
    (Collaborator - Nidhi Pal)
  • Hoshangabad Sarita Mahotsav : 3 day festival to explore science, sociology, art & culture of Narmada river | Place based learning
  • Time Exhibition : Art installation - Exhibition ‘A Matter of Time - an exploration to see time beyond clock and money’ 11th Feb 2024 | Project based learning
    (Collaborator - Nidhi Pal)
posts.plaintext:
* Food Camp : 3 day residential camp with children to explore science, sociology, art - culture of central theme Food. Transdisciplinary Approach
 * Voice of Vishwamitri : Group of teen age children studied Vishwamitri river in Vadodara, Gujarat and presented their work in Climate Festival, BLR as Geo inquiry Project.
   (Collaborator - Nidhi Pal)
 * Hoshangabad Sarita Mahotsav : 3 day festival to explore science, sociology, art & culture of Narmada river | Place based learning
 * Time Exhibition : Art installation - Exhibition ‘A Matter of Time - an exploration to see time beyond clock and money’ 11th Feb 2024 | Project based learning
   (Collaborator - Nidhi Pal)

posts.html:
• Nature Detective (Ages 3–10):
    • An immersive program blending nature exploration, movement, and the arts to spark early curiosity.
  • Local Khoji (Ages 11+):
    • A project-based inquiry program that empowers young adults to explore their local environment through "Geo-inquiry" and critical action.
posts.plaintext:
* Nature Detective (Ages 3–10):
   * An immersive program blending nature exploration, movement, and the arts to spark early curiosity.
 * Local Khoji (Ages 11+):
   * A project-based inquiry program that empowers young adults to explore their local environment through "Geo-inquiry" and critical action.

posts.html:
• we do tailor made workshops & capacity building sessions
  • we help to develop curriculum & activity modules on following topics : 
  1. Project based learning, Experiential learning 
  2. Nature Education, Environmental Education 
  3. Design Education, learning for 21st Century skills
  4. Language pedagogy, children literature, library program 
  5. Child development, learning and pedagogy
  6. Life skills education, Theater in Education 
  7. Self directed learning, Non formal learning spaces, development of ’third’ space
posts.plaintext:
* we do tailor made workshops & capacity building sessions
 * we help to develop curriculum & activity modules on following topics : 

 1. Project based learning, Experiential learning 
 2. Nature Education, Environmental Education 
 3. Design Education, learning for 21st Century skills
 4. Language pedagogy, children literature, library program 
 5. Child development, learning and pedagogy
 6. Life skills education, Theater in Education 
 7. Self directed learning, Non formal learning spaces, development of ’third’ space

posts.plaintext:
* Nature Detective (Ages 3–10): An immersive program blending nature exploration, movement, and the arts to spark early curiosity.
 * Local Khoji (Ages 11+): A project-based inquiry program that empowers young adults to explore their local environment through "Geo-inquiry" and critical action.
